title: Crispy Tangy Ethiopian Peanuts Stew
description: A hearty, low‑FODMAP stew bursting with tangy flavors and crispy roasted peanuts, perfect for a hangover‑cure and served over rice or injera.

ingredients.name: garlic‑infused oil
ingredients.notes: oil infused with garlic cloves, removed before using

instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F) and spread 1/2 cup roasted peanuts on a baking sheet; roast for 10 minutes until golden and fragrant, then set aside.
While peanuts roast, dice carrots, bell pepper, cucumber, and halve cherry tomatoes.
In a medium pot, heat garlic‑infused oil over medium heat. Add minced ginger and cook until fragrant, about 30 seconds.
Add diced carrots and bell pepper to the pot; sauté for 5 minutes until slightly softened.
Stir in diced cucumber and cherry tomatoes; cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Add the roasted peanuts and peanut butter to the pot, stirring until the peanuts coat the vegetables.
Pour in peanut oil and red wine vinegar; stir in ground coriander and salt, mixing thoroughly.
Reduce heat to low and let the stew simmer for 15 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld together.
Taste and adjust seasoning if necessary. Just before serving, sprinkle chopped cilantro over the stew.
Serve the crispy tangy stew over cooked white rice or on top of warm injera, and enjoy.
